input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: I need to specify jobs for both runtimes explicitly. Let me update the benchmark file:
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet needs to be run from a single TFM, but it can use `[SimpleJob]` with runtime moniker to target multiple runtimes. Let me adjust the approach - I'll use BenchmarkDotNet's Job attributes to specify both runtimes, and run from one TFM.

Actually, the simpler approach is to just run it from one framework and use `[SimpleJob(RuntimeMoniker.Net90)]` and `[SimpleJob(RuntimeMoniker.Net100)]` attributes.

Let me check what RuntimeMoniker is available for .NET 10.

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-801844e5704a4da2957e04af41e3c947/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks && dotnet run -c Release -f net10.0 -- --join --filter '*' 2>&1", "description": "Run benchmarks net9 vs net10", "initial_wait": 300}

import zipfile, re, os, glob

# search the XML doc for Net10
for p in glob.glob(os.path.expanduser('~/.nuget/packages/benchmarkdotnet/0.14.0/lib/net8.0/BenchmarkDotNet.xml')):
    with open(p) as f:
        content = f.read()
    matches = re.findall(r'Net\d+', content)
    for m in sorted(set(matches)):
        print(m)
"
